title: UM171 and StemRegenin-1 synergize to expand human cord blood HSCs with durable multilineage engraftment
abstract: Ex vivo expansion of hematopoietic stem cells (HSCs) from cord blood is limited by rapid differentiation. We show that combined treatment with UM171 and StemRegenin-1 (SR1) for 7 days expands CD34+CD45RA- HSCs 180-fold while preserving long-term multilineage engraftment in xenograft models. Single-cell transcriptomics revealed suppression of myeloid priming programs and maintenance of self-renewal signatures. Phase I trial data show durable trilineage engraftment in 8 of 9 patients at 12 months.
authors: Fares I, Chagraoui J, Gareau Y, et al.
notes: Core HSC expansion — directly in primary focus area

title: CD33-targeting CAR-T cells incorporating a dominant-negative TGF-beta receptor overcome immunosuppression in relapsed AML
abstract: Relapsed/refractory acute myeloid leukemia (AML) remains difficult to treat with CAR-T cells due to myelosuppression and TGF-beta-mediated immunosuppression in the bone marrow niche. We engineered CD33-targeting CAR-T cells expressing a dominant-negative TGF-beta receptor II, demonstrating superior persistence and AML clearance in patient-derived xenograft models. A phase I/II trial in 15 relapsed AML patients showed 53% complete remission rate with manageable CRS.
authors: Wang Y, Bhatt DL, Riviere I, et al.

title: Adenine base editing in HSCs achieves near-complete correction of sickle cell disease without off-target genotoxicity
abstract: Sickle cell disease (SCD) results from a single A-to-T transversion in HBB. Using adenine base editors delivered by lipid nanoparticles, we achieved 94% correction efficiency in mobilized CD34+ HSCs with <0.05% indel formation. Corrected cells maintained long-term engraftment in xenograft models. We report 12-month follow-up data from the first 6 patients in a phase I trial showing sustained HbA production and resolution of vaso-occlusive crises.
authors: Newby GA, Yen JS, Zhao KT, et al.
notes: Base editing in HSCs for hemoglobinopathy — core topic

title: Off-the-shelf iPSC-derived NK cells with anti-CD38 CAR show durable responses in multiple myeloma
abstract: Allogeneic cell therapies require scalable manufacturing from a single donor source. We generated anti-CD38 CAR-NK cells from iPSCs using a 14-day differentiation protocol, yielding >10^9 cells per manufacturing run. iPSC-NK cells demonstrated superior metabolic fitness and resistance to myeloma-induced exhaustion versus peripheral blood NK cells. In a phase I trial, 7 of 12 patients with relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma achieved partial response or better with no graft-versus-host disease.
authors: Liu E, Marin D, Banerjee P, et al.

title: De novo design of TCR-mimic antibodies targeting HLA-A2/WT1 using RFdiffusion and ProteinMPNN
abstract: T-cell receptor mimic (TCRm) antibodies that recognize peptide-MHC complexes can redirect immune responses without the need for cellular therapies. We used RFdiffusion to generate backbone scaffolds targeting the shallow HLA-A2/WT1 groove, followed by ProteinMPNN sequence design and AlphaFold2 validation. The top candidates showed KD < 10 nM binding and redirected T-cell killing of AML blasts in co-culture assays.
authors: Bennett NR, Watson JL, Ragotte RJ, et al.
notes: AI protein design applied to immunotherapy target — highly relevant

title: mRNA-LNP delivery of Cas9 and chemically modified sgRNA achieves transient gene editing in human CD34+ cells with minimal innate immune activation
abstract: Electroporation-based delivery of ribonucleoprotein (RNP) complexes for CRISPR editing in HSCs causes significant cell stress. We optimized lipid nanoparticle (LNP) formulations for co-delivery of mRNA-encoded Cas9 and chemically modified sgRNA targeting BCL11A enhancer in CD34+ cells. LNP delivery achieved 78% editing efficiency with 40% reduced apoptosis versus RNP electroporation, and edited cells showed equivalent engraftment in xenografts.
authors: Kenjo E, Hozumi H, Makita Y, et al.
notes: Delivery method for HSC gene editing — manufacturing-relevant

title: Single-cell atlas of human bone marrow reveals niche-specific HSPC states lost in myelodysplastic syndrome
abstract: The human bone marrow niche shapes HSPC fate decisions through spatially organized signaling. We generated a single-cell multi-omic atlas of 500,000 bone marrow cells from 40 donors spanning healthy aging and MDS. We identified 8 HSC sub-states defined by distinct niche proximity signatures. In MDS, HSCs accumulate in a pro-myeloid state associated with loss of CXCL12-CXCR4 axis signaling, providing a molecular basis for impaired HSC self-renewal.
authors: Weinreb C, Rodriguez-Fraticelli A, Camargo FD, et al.
notes: HSC niche biology in MDS — relevant to expansion and engraftment context

title: Regulatory T cell co-infusion reduces CAR-T toxicity while preserving anti-tumor efficacy in large B-cell lymphoma
abstract: Cytokine release syndrome (CRS) and immune effector cell-associated neurotoxicity (ICANS) remain barriers to CAR-T therapy. We tested co-infusion of polyclonal Tregs with axicabtagene ciloleucel in 20 patients with relapsed LBCL. Treg co-infusion reduced grade 3+ CRS from 43% to 15% and ICANS from 38% to 10% without reducing 6-month complete response rates. Mechanistically, Tregs blunted early IL-6 and IFN-gamma surges without inhibiting CAR-T expansion.
authors: Schietinger A, Delrow JJ, Basom RS, et al.

title: T cell exhaustion is driven by TCF7+ progenitor depletion in the tumor microenvironment
abstract: Exhausted T cells in solid tumors lose effector function progressively. We show that TCF7+ progenitor-exhausted T cells are the primary source of effector T cell renewal, and that depletion of this population correlates with anti-PD-1 resistance. CXCR5-targeted checkpoint blockade preferentially expands TCF7+ progenitors over terminally exhausted cells. These findings suggest a two-phase model of T cell exhaustion with implications for combination immunotherapy.
authors: Miller BC, Sen DR, Al Abosy R, et al.
notes: T cell exhaustion — relevant to CAR-T persistence but not HSC focus

title: Cryo-EM structure of the spliceosome reveals mechanism of SF3B1 mutation in myelodysplastic syndrome
abstract: SF3B1 mutations are the most common somatic mutations in MDS and drive aberrant splicing of ABCB7 and other transcripts. We determined a 2.9A cryo-EM structure of the human spliceosome bound to wild-type and K700E mutant SF3B1, revealing how the mutation alters branch point adenosine recognition. A small molecule that stabilizes wild-type SF3B1 conformation partially restored normal splicing in patient-derived MDS cells.
authors: Cretu C, Agrawal AA, Cook A, et al.
notes: MDS mechanism — tangentially relevant, mainly structural biology

title: Cardiac progenitor cell transplantation improves ejection fraction after myocardial infarction: a randomized controlled trial
abstract: Stem cell therapy for ischemic heart disease has shown mixed results in prior trials. We conducted a double-blind RCT of 150 patients randomized to intracoronary c-Kit+ cardiac progenitor cells or placebo after myocardial infarction. At 12 months, cell-treated patients showed a 5.8% improvement in LVEF versus 2.1% for placebo (p=0.03). No serious adverse events were attributed to cell therapy.
authors: Bolli R, Chugh AR, D'Amario D, et al.
notes: Cardiac stem cells — unrelated field, not HSC/hematology

title: Cerebral organoids model cortical development and reveal SOX2 dosage sensitivity in microcephaly
abstract: Human cerebral organoids derived from iPSCs recapitulate key features of cortical development including ventricular zone formation and radial migration. We used CRISPR interference to titrate SOX2 expression and show that a 30% reduction is sufficient to cause microcephaly-like phenotypes. Single-cell RNA-seq of 80,000 cells across organoid stages identified a neural progenitor subtype absent in SOX2-reduced organoids.
authors: Lancaster MA, Renner M, Martin CA, et al.
notes: Neural organoids — iPSC but completely unrelated field

title: A global analysis of SARS-CoV-2 vaccine effectiveness against hospitalization: systematic review and meta-analysis
abstract: COVID-19 vaccine effectiveness wanes over time and varies by variant. We pooled data from 47 studies across 22 countries (n=3.2M) to estimate vaccine effectiveness against hospitalization by vaccine type, variant, and time since vaccination. mRNA vaccines showed 87% effectiveness at 3 months declining to 64% at 9 months. Booster doses restored effectiveness to 91%. These findings support ongoing booster recommendations for high-risk populations.
authors: Ioannidis JPA, Ziegler PE, Madoff LC, et al.

title: Structure-guided optimization of BCL-2 inhibitors overcomes venetoclax resistance in chronic lymphocytic leukemia
abstract: Venetoclax resistance in CLL is mediated by upregulation of BCL-XL and MCL-1. Using cryo-EM structures of BCL-2 family members in complex with resistant CLL cells, we identified a cryptic binding pocket exploited by a next-generation PROTAC degrader of BCL-XL. The degrader synergized with ibrutinib to overcome venetoclax resistance in 8 patient-derived CLL models and showed tolerability in a 6-patient dose escalation.
authors: Seymour JF, Ma S, Brander DM, et al.
notes: CLL treatment but small molecule only — no cell/gene therapy angle

title: Automated GMP-compatible microbioreactor system enables closed-process manufacturing of CAR-T cells with reduced inter-batch variability
abstract: CAR-T cell manufacturing requires multiple open-process steps that introduce variability and contamination risk. We developed a fully automated, closed-system microbioreactor that integrates lentiviral transduction, expansion, and formulation in a single device. In 12 consecutive manufacturing runs, the system achieved >95% process success rate with inter-batch variability in transduction efficiency of 4.2% versus 18.7% for conventional flask-based manufacturing.
authors: Vormittag P, Gunn R, Ghorashian S, et al.
notes: GMP manufacturing for CAR-T — directly in focus area
